British ITV television series

This article is about the ITV series. For the fictional character, see [Miss Marple](/wiki/Miss_Marple "Miss Marple"). For the 1980s BBC series starring Joan Hickson, see [Miss Marple (TV series)](/wiki/Miss_Marple_(TV_series) "Miss Marple (TV series)").

***Agatha Christie's Marple*** (or simply ***Marple***) is a British [ITV](/wiki/ITV_(TV_network) "ITV (TV network)") [television](/wiki/Television "Television") programme loosely based on books and short stories by British crime novelist [Agatha Christie](/wiki/Agatha_Christie "Agatha Christie"). [The title character](/wiki/Miss_Marple "Miss Marple") was played by [Geraldine McEwan](/wiki/Geraldine_McEwan "Geraldine McEwan") from the first to the third series, until her retirement from the role, and by [Julia McKenzie](/wiki/Julia_McKenzie "Julia McKenzie") from the fourth series onwards. Unlike the counterpart TV series *[Agatha Christie's Poirot](/wiki/Agatha_Christie%27s_Poirot "Agatha Christie's Poirot")*, the show took many liberties with Christie’s works, most notably adding Miss Marple’s character to the adaptations of novels in which she never appeared. Following the conclusion of the sixth series, the BBC acquired the rights for the production of Agatha Christie adaptations, suggesting that ITV would be unable to make a seventh series of *Marple*.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Premise

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Agatha_Christie%27s_Marple&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Premise")]

Main article: [List of *Agatha Christie's Marple* episodes](/wiki/List_of_Agatha_Christie%27s_Marple_episodes "List of Agatha Christie's Marple episodes")

*Agatha Christie's Marple* follows the adventures of Miss Jane Marple, an elderly spinster living in the quiet little village of [St. Mary Mead](/wiki/St._Mary_Mead "St. Mary Mead"). During her many visits to friends and relatives in other villages (and sometimes when simply being at home), Miss Marple often stumbles upon or hears about mysterious murders, which she helps solve. Although the police are sometimes reluctant to accept Miss Marple's help, her reputation and unparalleled powers of observation eventually win them over.

During her adventures, Miss Marple is aided by close friends, relatives, or other allies that she meets, which include [Tommy and Tuppence](/wiki/Tommy_and_Tuppence "Tommy and Tuppence") (protagonists of another series of Christie novels).[[3]](#cite_note-3)

## Cast and characters

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Agatha_Christie%27s_Marple&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Cast and characters")]

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This section **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Agatha_Christie%27s_Marple "Special:EditPage/Agatha Christie's Marple") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners") in this section. Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *(May 2024)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

The only character to appear in every episode is Miss Marple herself, played by [Geraldine McEwan](/wiki/Geraldine_McEwan "Geraldine McEwan") through series 3 and by [Julia McKenzie](/wiki/Julia_McKenzie "Julia McKenzie") from series 4 onwards.[[4]](#cite_note-4)[[5]](#cite_note-5)

A few other characters appear in more than one episode, although they are not always played by the same actor. Exceptions are [Joanna Lumley](/wiki/Joanna_Lumley "Joanna Lumley"), who plays Mrs Dolly Bantry in *The Body in the Library* and *The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side*,[[6]](#cite_note-6) and [Stephen Churchett](/wiki/Stephen_Churchett "Stephen Churchett"), who appears as the coroner in four episodes.

Dr Haydock appears in three episodes, but is played by three actors: [Robin Soans](/wiki/Robin_Soans "Robin Soans") in *The Body in the Library*, [Robert Powell](/wiki/Robert_Powell "Robert Powell") in *The Murder at the Vicarage* and [Neil Stuke](/wiki/Neil_Stuke "Neil Stuke") in *The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side*. Jason Rafiel, an old friend of Marple's, was voiced by [Herbert Lom](/wiki/Herbert_Lom "Herbert Lom") (who also made an on-screen appearance as Mr Dufosse in *The Murder at the Vicarage*) in *Nemesis* and played by [Antony Sher](/wiki/Antony_Sher "Antony Sher") in *A Caribbean Mystery*.

The series also featured real-life characters: [Winston Churchill](/wiki/Winston_Churchill "Winston Churchill") appeared in *[The Sittaford Mystery](/wiki/The_Sittaford_Mystery "The Sittaford Mystery")*, [Noël Coward](/wiki/No%C3%ABl_Coward "Noël Coward") in *[4.50 from Paddington](/wiki/4.50_from_Paddington "4.50 from Paddington")*, [Louis Armstrong](/wiki/Louis_Armstrong "Louis Armstrong") in *[At Bertram's Hotel](/wiki/At_Bertram%27s_Hotel "At Bertram's Hotel")*, and [Ian Fleming](/wiki/Ian_Fleming "Ian Fleming") and [James Bond](/wiki/James_Bond_(ornithologist) "James Bond (ornithologist)") in *[A Caribbean Mystery](/wiki/A_Caribbean_Mystery "A Caribbean Mystery")*.

## Episodes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Agatha_Christie%27s_Marple&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Episodes")]

Main article: [List of Agatha Christie's Marple episodes](/wiki/List_of_Agatha_Christie%27s_Marple_episodes "List of Agatha Christie's Marple episodes")

Each series consists of four feature-length episodes, except series six which has only three episodes. The first six episodes were all adaptations of *[Miss Marple](/wiki/Miss_Marple "Miss Marple")* novels by Christie. Subsequent episodes were derived from works featuring Miss Marple and Christie novels that did not feature the character. The title of the series removes the word *Miss* from [Miss Marple](/wiki/Miss_Marple "Miss Marple"), to match the title of *[Agatha Christie's Poirot](/wiki/Agatha_Christie%27s_Poirot "Agatha Christie's Poirot")* series.[[7]](#cite_note-7)

*Marple* was filmed in various locations, including [London](/wiki/London "London"), the villages of [Englefield](/wiki/Englefield,_Berkshire "Englefield, Berkshire") in [Berkshire](/wiki/Berkshire "Berkshire"), [Chilham](/wiki/Chilham "Chilham") in [Kent](/wiki/Kent "Kent"), [Turville](/wiki/Turville "Turville") in [Buckinghamshire](/wiki/Buckinghamshire "Buckinghamshire") and [Blewbury](/wiki/Blewbury "Blewbury") in [Oxfordshire](/wiki/Oxfordshire "Oxfordshire").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

[Windsor Guildhall](/wiki/Windsor_Guildhall "Windsor Guildhall") in [Windsor](/wiki/Windsor,_Berkshire "Windsor, Berkshire") was featured as the fictional Melchester in *[Murder at the Vicarage](/wiki/Murder_at_the_Vicarage "Murder at the Vicarage")*,[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]. [Knebworth House](/wiki/Knebworth_House "Knebworth House") in Hertfordshire was used as the exterior of Rutherford Hall in *[4.50 From Paddington](/wiki/4.50_From_Paddington "4.50 From Paddington")*, while [Highclere Castle](/wiki/Highclere_Castle "Highclere Castle") was used for the interior shots [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]. [Knebworth House](/wiki/Knebworth_House "Knebworth House") was also used in *[Greenshaw's Folly](/wiki/Greenshaw%27s_Folly "Greenshaw's Folly")*.[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] [Hatfield House](/wiki/Hatfield_House "Hatfield House") was used as Chimneys in *[The Secret of Chimneys](/wiki/The_Secret_of_Chimneys "The Secret of Chimneys")*, and also in *[Greenshaw's Folly](/wiki/Greenshaw%27s_Folly "Greenshaw's Folly")* [*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*].

[Hambleden](/wiki/Hambleden "Hambleden") was used as [St Mary Mead](/wiki/St_Mary_Mead "St Mary Mead") and [Dorney Court](/wiki/Dorney_Court "Dorney Court") featured in *[The Body in the Library](/wiki/The_Body_in_the_Library "The Body in the Library")* as Gossington Hall, home of the Bantrys, and also as the vicarage in "The Moving Finger" [Fawley Court](/wiki/Fawley_Court "Fawley Court") in Buckinghamshire was used as Stoneygates in *[They Do It with Mirrors](/wiki/They_Do_It_with_Mirrors "They Do It with Mirrors")*. [The Homewood](/wiki/The_Homewood "The Homewood") in Surrey was used as Michael and Ellie's house in *[Endless Night](/wiki/Endless_Night_(novel) "Endless Night (novel)")*, while The Grotto was used as the exterior of their secret folly.[*[clarification needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Please_clarify "Wikipedia:Please clarify")*][[8]](#cite_note-dicamillocompanion-8)

In *[Why Didn't They Ask Evans?](/wiki/Why_Didn%27t_They_Ask_Evans%3F "Why Didn't They Ask Evans?")*, the Castle Savage scenes were largely filmed at [Loseley Park](/wiki/Loseley_Park "Loseley Park") near [Guildford](/wiki/Guildford "Guildford").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] *[A Caribbean Mystery](/wiki/A_Caribbean_Mystery "A Caribbean Mystery")* was filmed in [Cape Town](/wiki/Cape_Town "Cape Town"), South Africa, although the beach scenes were shot at [Boulders Beach](/wiki/Boulders_Beach "Boulders Beach").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] For the production based on *[Endless Night](/wiki/Endless_Night_(novel) "Endless Night (novel)")*, scenes were filmed in [Dorchester](/wiki/Dorchester,_Dorset "Dorchester, Dorset").[[9]](#cite_note-9)

The exterior of Marina Gregg's house in *[The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side](/wiki/The_Mirror_Crack%27d_from_Side_to_Side "The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side")* were filmed at [North Mymms Park](/wiki/North_Mymms "North Mymms"),[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*] and the cemetery in *[Nemesis](/wiki/Nemesis_(Christie_novel) "Nemesis (Christie novel)")* was filmed at [Waverley Abbey](/wiki/Waverley_Abbey "Waverley Abbey").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

Christie's twelve novels featuring Miss Marple were all adapted for the series. *[The Murder at the Vicarage](/wiki/The_Murder_at_the_Vicarage "The Murder at the Vicarage")*, *[The Body in the Library](/wiki/The_Body_in_the_Library "The Body in the Library")*, *[4.50 from Paddington](/wiki/4.50_from_Paddington "4.50 from Paddington")*, and *[A Murder is Announced](/wiki/A_Murder_is_Announced "A Murder is Announced")* in Series 1, *[Sleeping Murder](/wiki/Sleeping_Murder "Sleeping Murder")* and *[The Moving Finger](/wiki/The_Moving_Finger "The Moving Finger")* in Series 2, *[At Bertram's Hotel](/wiki/At_Bertram%27s_Hotel "At Bertram's Hotel")* and *[Nemesis](/wiki/Nemesis_(Christie_novel) "Nemesis (Christie novel)")* in Series 3, *[A Pocket Full of Rye](/wiki/A_Pocket_Full_of_Rye "A Pocket Full of Rye")* and *[They Do It with Mirrors](/wiki/They_Do_It_with_Mirrors "They Do It with Mirrors")* in Series 4, *[The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side](/wiki/The_Mirror_Crack%27d_from_Side_to_Side "The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side")* in Series 5 and *[A Caribbean Mystery](/wiki/A_Caribbean_Mystery "A Caribbean Mystery")* in Series 6.

In addition, several short stories featuring [Miss Marple](/wiki/Miss_Marple "Miss Marple") were adapted into full-length episodes across the series. *[The Blue Geranium](/wiki/The_Thirteen_Problems#The_Blue_Geranium "The Thirteen Problems")* in Series 5, elements of *[The Herb of Death](/wiki/The_Thirteen_Problems#The_Herb_of_Death "The Thirteen Problems")* were incorporated into the adaptation of *[The Secret of Chimneys](/wiki/The_Secret_of_Chimneys "The Secret of Chimneys")* in Series 5, while *[Greenshaw's Folly](/wiki/Greenshaw%27s_Folly "Greenshaw's Folly")* and *[The Thumb Mark of St. Peter](/wiki/The_Thumb_Mark_of_St._Peter "The Thumb Mark of St. Peter")* were combined into one story for Series 6.

Across the twenty-three adaptations, many changes have been made from the source material.

* *[The Body in the Library](/wiki/The_Body_in_the_Library "The Body in the Library")* introduces a lesbian affair and changes the identity of one of the killers.
* *[The Murder at the Vicarage](/wiki/The_Murder_at_the_Vicarage "The Murder at the Vicarage")* removes or changes some minor characters and manufactures Miss Marple's early life.
* *[4.50 from Paddington](/wiki/4.50_from_Paddington "4.50 from Paddington")* removes one character's death and simplifies the killer's motive.
* *[A Murder Is Announced](/wiki/A_Murder_Is_Announced "A Murder Is Announced")* changes some of the characters and makes an implied lesbian relationship an explicit one.
* *[Sleeping Murder](/wiki/Sleeping_Murder "Sleeping Murder")* changes the killer's motive, some characters backstory, inserts a central romantic relationship, and sideplot involving a travelling band of singers.
* *[The Moving Finger](/wiki/The_Moving_Finger "The Moving Finger")* changes the time period and some character backstory, including the addition of a suicide attempt by the narrating character and two other characters in a gay relationship; otherwise, it is largely faithful to the source material.
* *[By the Pricking of My Thumbs](/wiki/By_the_Pricking_of_My_Thumbs "By the Pricking of My Thumbs")* inserts Miss Marple into what was originally a Tommy and Tuppence novel, and therefore changes many plot elements, including Tommy and Tuppence's relationship, adding characters, subplots, and changing the time period.
* *[The Sittaford Mystery](/wiki/The_Sittaford_Mystery "The Sittaford Mystery")* is very loosely based on the novel (which does not feature Miss Marple) and changes the identity of the killer.
* *[At Bertram's Hotel](/wiki/At_Bertram%27s_Hotel "At Bertram's Hotel")* is only loosely based on the novel, changing many elements of the plot, characters and time period.
* *[Ordeal by Innocence](/wiki/Ordeal_by_Innocence "Ordeal by Innocence")* includes significant changes to the characters, adding Marple into the story.
* *[Towards Zero](/wiki/Towards_Zero "Towards Zero")* inserts Miss Marple into the story and changes some characters, but is largely true to the original novel.
* *[Nemesis](/wiki/Nemesis_(Christie_novel) "Nemesis (Christie novel)")* is only loosely based on the novel, and changes characters and setting.
* *[A Pocket Full of Rye](/wiki/A_Pocket_Full_of_Rye "A Pocket Full of Rye")* is a faithful adaptation of the novel, with only minor changes to the way characters are described.
* *[Murder Is Easy](/wiki/Murder_Is_Easy "Murder Is Easy")* is very loosely based on the novel which does not feature Miss Marple, also changing the murderer's motive.
* *[They Do It with Mirrors](/wiki/They_Do_It_with_Mirrors "They Do It with Mirrors")* combines some characters and adds an arson attack.
* *[Why Didn't They Ask Evans?](/wiki/Why_Didn%27t_They_Ask_Evans%3F "Why Didn't They Ask Evans?")* is only loosely based on the novel, which does not feature Miss Marple, changing the plot and characters.
* *[The Pale Horse](/wiki/The_Pale_Horse "The Pale Horse")* is very loosely based on the novel which does not feature Miss Marple.
* *[The Secret of Chimneys](/wiki/The_Secret_of_Chimneys "The Secret of Chimneys")* is very loosely based on the novel which does not feature Miss Marple, although it uses story elements from *[The Herb of Death](/wiki/The_Thirteen_Problems#The_Herb_of_Death "The Thirteen Problems")*. It also changes the killer's identity.
* *[The Blue Geranium](/wiki/The_Thirteen_Problems "The Thirteen Problems")* is greatly embellished from the original short story.
* *[The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side](/wiki/The_Mirror_Crack%27d_from_Side_to_Side "The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side")* keeps closely to the original story.
* *[A Caribbean Mystery](/wiki/A_Caribbean_Mystery "A Caribbean Mystery")* keeps closely to the original story, apart from the inclusion of real-life novelist [Ian Fleming](/wiki/Ian_Fleming "Ian Fleming") and ornithologist [James Bond](/wiki/James_Bond_(ornithologist) "James Bond (ornithologist)").
* *[Greenshaw's Folly](/wiki/Greenshaw%27s_Folly "Greenshaw's Folly")* combines the short story with elements from *[The Thumb Mark of St. Peter](/wiki/The_Thumb_Mark_of_St._Peter "The Thumb Mark of St. Peter")*. The story is embellished, but keeps to the core of the original works.
* *[Endless Night](/wiki/Endless_Night_(novel) "Endless Night (novel)")* is a faithful adaptation of the novel despite Miss Marple being added, but the story is very similar to *[The Case of The Caretaker](/wiki/Miss_Marple%27s_Final_Cases_and_Two_Other_Stories#The_Case_of_The_Caretaker "Miss Marple's Final Cases and Two Other Stories").*

*Agatha Christie's Marple* was originally aired in the United States on [PBS](/wiki/PBS "PBS") within the anthology series *[Mystery!](/wiki/Mystery! "Mystery!")*, where it is presented as *Agatha Christie's **"Miss** Marple"*, before airing on the anthology series *[Masterpiece Theatre](/wiki/Masterpiece_(TV_series) "Masterpiece (TV series)")*.[[10]](#cite_note-10) The series is broadcast to the whole of Canada on [CBC](/wiki/Canadian_Broadcasting_Corporation "Canadian Broadcasting Corporation") and in French on [Radio-Canada](/wiki/Canadian_Broadcasting_Corporation "Canadian Broadcasting Corporation"). In Australia, *Agatha Christie's Marple* airs on [ABC1](/wiki/Australian_Broadcasting_Corporation "Australian Broadcasting Corporation").

*Marple* is also being broadcast on [ATV World](/wiki/ATV_World "ATV World") in [Hong Kong](/wiki/Hong_Kong "Hong Kong"), on *EBS* and [MegaTV](/wiki/MegaTV_(Korea) "MegaTV (Korea)") in [South Korea](/wiki/South_Korea "South Korea") and on [CCTV-8](/wiki/CCTV-8 "CCTV-8") in China. China, however, refused to show *The Body in the Library* and *Murder Is Easy,* due to the involvement of a lesbian and an incestuous relationship respectively. *Why Didn't They Ask Evans?* was extensively edited, due to the involvement of War-period China.

In Norway, the series has been airing on state broadcaster [NRK1](/wiki/NRK1 "NRK1") as "Miss Marple". In [Sweden](/wiki/Sweden "Sweden"), *Marple* airs on [TV4](/wiki/TV4_(Sweden) "TV4 (Sweden)"), the biggest commercial TV station. In Poland, the series airs on [Ale Kino+](/wiki/Ale_Kino%2B "Ale Kino+"). In the Czech Republic it is broadcast under the title *Slečna Marplová*, Czech for "Miss Marple".

In Brazil, the series airs on [HBO Brasil](/wiki/HBO_Brasil "HBO Brasil"). In the Netherlands it is broadcast on [BBC First](/wiki/BBC_First_(Dutch_TV_channel) "BBC First (Dutch TV channel)").

### Awards and nominations

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Agatha_Christie%27s_Marple&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Awards and nominations")]

In 2005, Geraldine McEwan was nominated for a [Satellite Award for Best Actress – Miniseries or Television Film](/wiki/Satellite_Award_for_Best_Actress_%E2%80%93_Miniseries_or_Television_Film "Satellite Award for Best Actress – Miniseries or Television Film") for her performance in Series 1.[[12]](#cite_note-12) The series was also nominated for a [Primetime Emmy Award](/wiki/Primetime_Emmy_Awards "Primetime Emmy Awards") for Outstanding Special Class Program, in 2005.[[13]](#cite_note-13) In 2006, series composer, Dominik Scherrer was the winner of a Gold Medal at the [New York Festivals](/wiki/New_York_Festivals "New York Festivals") for TV Programming & Promotion - Craft: Program - Best Original Music/Lyrics, as well as receiving an [Ivor Novello Award](/wiki/Ivor_Novello_Awards "Ivor Novello Awards") nomination for Best Television Soundtrack, in 2011.[[14]](#cite_note-14) The series was Inducted into the Online Film & Television Association – Television Hall of Fame: Productions, in 2014.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

## Home media

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Agatha_Christie%27s_Marple&action=edit&section=10 "Edit section: Home media")]

In the United States, *Agatha Christie's Marple* was made available on DVD from [Acorn Media](/wiki/Acorn_DVD "Acorn DVD"), where the entire series is available in individual sets, and a set containing the first three series. The series has been released in its entirety, in both individual and complete sets, in the UK, via [ITV DVD](/wiki/ITV_DVD "ITV DVD"), and in Australia, originally from [ABC DVD](/wiki/ABC_Commercial "ABC Commercial"), then [Roadshow Entertainment](/wiki/Roadshow_Entertainment "Roadshow Entertainment"). The series will, for the first time, receive a Blu-ray release, consisting of the first three series in a limited edition set from ViaVision in Australia, in 2024. In Germany, the DVDs were distributed by Polyband/WVG in individual sets, a complete limited edition set, followed by a complete standard edition set.
